title: psoralea purpurea
intro: Psoralea purpurea, also known as Indian turnip or prairie turnip, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. Traditionally, Psoralea purpurea has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including improving digestion, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of Psoralea purpurea are often made from the dried roots or leaves, and can be used in teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
